message:
fix(btree): prevent trailing orphan divider in interior page distribution and clean up idioms

Interior B-tree rebalancing (balance.rs):
- Fix a bug in compute_interior_distribution where a single trailing
  cell could be consumed as an interior divider with no payload cell
  remaining for the right-most child page. When exactly one cell trails
  after the current page boundary, the distribution now backtracks one
  cell so that two cells remain: one promoted as the divider and one
  placed as payload on the right page. This prevents database corruption
  from pages that violate the invariant that every interior page must
  have at least one payload cell.
- Return a DatabaseCorrupt error if backtracking would leave zero cells,
  catching an impossible-to-recover state early.
- Update existing test call-sites to pass BtreePageType enum variants
  instead of bare booleans, matching an earlier API change to
  compute_distribution that replaced the `is_leaf: bool` parameter with
  a typed BtreePageType.
- Add new test test_distribution_interior_avoids_trailing_orphan_divider
  that exercises the specific edge case of three cells where the third
  is large enough to push the distribution boundary, verifying the
  backtrack logic produces [1, 1] with one promoted divider.

BeTree (be_tree.rs):
- Replace `.filter(|v| v.is_some()).count()` with the more idiomatic
  `.flatten().count()` in the len() method. Both are semantically
  identical for Option<V> iterators, but flatten is clearer intent.

Cursor (cursor.rs):
- Reformat the unreachable!() macro call in binary_search_index_interior
  match arm to comply with rustfmt line-length conventions. No logic
  change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
